title: Scaphyglottis corallorrhiza

herb_names_common.name: scaphyglottis corallorrhiza is not commonly referred to by a name other than its botanical name

what_description: Scaphyglottis corallorrhiza is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is a terrestrial or epiphytic herb with a short, stout stem and a single, apical leaf that is lanceolate to elliptic in shape, 6-12 cm long, and 1.5-3.5 cm wide. The leaf is leathery, dark green, and has a petiole 0.5-2 cm long. The inflorescence is a raceme, 5-15 cm long, bearing 2-6 flowers that are resupinate, 3-4 cm long, and 1-2 cm wide, with a yellowish-brown sepals and petals, and a white, minutely papillose labellum. The flowers have a distinctive coralline shape, which is the source of the species name.

properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Scaphyglottis corallorrhiza are its antimicrobial activity, which is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and saponins that inhibit the growth of various microorganisms. preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Scaphyglottis corallorrhiza are tincture, infusion, tea, salve, and decoction. A tincture of Scaphyglottis corallorrhiza is prepared by soaking the plant in a solvent like ethanol or glycerin to create a concentrated liquid extract. An infusion is prepared by steeping the plant material in hot water to release its bioactive compounds. Scaphyglottis corallorrhiza tea is made by infusing the plant material in boiling water, while a salve is created by mixing the plant extract with a carrier oil or wax.
